w:r.w:t: n8n · Groq/Llama 3 · PostgreSQL · Google Sheets · Gmail/SMTP
Production-grade · $0.00 / month · Optimized for 8 GB RAM Laptop
Operational Resilience — Rate Limiting, Tunnels & Fallback
Every component below is either fully open-source or operates on a permanent free tier with no credit-card requirement. Monthly operational expenditure: $0.00.
AGPL — unlimited workflows & nodes on self-host
15 GB storage, Sheets API 300 req/min free
Free for personal use — no open inbound ports
Ollama + Llama 3 8B Q4 (local — fits in 8 GB)

💡 Groq free tier gives 14,400 requests/day (reset at midnight UTC). This workflow uses 2 Groq calls per email (classify + draft). At that rate you can safely process 7,200 emails/day — well above a lean startup’s needs. A 3rd optional validation call (Section 6.2) further reduces hallucinations at the cost of halving throughput to ~4,800 emails/day.
2. System Architecture — ASCII Diagram & Data Flow
│ │ (Access n8n dashboard remotely — zero open firewall ports) │ │
n8n IMAP node polls mailbox every 60 seconds for UNSEEN messages.
Extracts: message_id, from, subject, body_text, thread_id, received_at.
n8n Postgres node: SELECT 1 FROM email_logs WHERE message_id = $1
If row MISSING → INSERT with status='processing' and continue.
Prompt: classify email → one of {spam, inquiry, urgent, lead, newsletter}
If needs_reply = false → mark status='no_reply_needed' and STOP.
Inject: company name, tone, FAQs, pricing, product summary.
Append last 3 emails in thread (from email_logs) for continuity.
Send enriched prompt → get JSON { subject, body, reasoning, confidence }
Append row to Google Sheets: [timestamp, from, subject, draft, status='PENDING']
A second n8n workflow polls the Sheet every 5 min.
If status = 'APPROVED' → trigger SMTP send workflow.
If status = 'EDIT' → use edited body from Sheets column G.
Rate limiter: max 1 send every 45 seconds (avoids Gmail spam flags).
All token usage written to email_logs.token_usage.
Create a project folder and add the following docker-compose.yml:
# No 'version' key needed — Docker Compose v2 ignores it (removed deprecated field)
N8N_EXECUTIONS_DATA_MAX_AGE: "168" # Prune execution logs older than 7 days (saves RAM on 8 GB system)
N8N_METRICS: "false" # Disable Prometheus metrics to reduce memory usage
Create a file named .env in the same folder as docker-compose.yml BEFORE running docker compose up. Never commit this file to git.
# On Windows (Krishna_pc), set these in PowerShell instead of chmod:
# Add to .gitignore to prevent accidental commits:
# 2. IMPORTANT: Set Docker Desktop RAM limit to 4 GB first
# Docker Desktop → Settings → Resources → Memory → 4 GB → Apply
# Login with credentials set in docker-compose.yml
# 5. View logs if something goes wrong
Search "App Passwords" → Create one named "n8n Email Bot"
Copy the 16-character password — you'll never see it again
Use this password in n8n IMAP/SMTP credential (NOT your main Gmail password)

⚠ Gmail free tier limits: 500 emails/day send via SMTP. For higher volume, use Zoho Mail free tier (500/day) as a second account, or rotate between multiple Gmail accounts via different n8n credential sets.
Sign up at https://console.groq.com (free — no credit card)
Navigate to API Keys → Create New Key
In n8n: Credentials → New → HTTP Header Auth
Header Name: Authorization | Value: Bearer gsk_YOUR_KEY_HERE
Enable "Google Sheets API" and "Google Drive API"
Create a Service Account → Download JSON key file
In n8n: Credentials → New → Google Sheets OAuth2 API → upload JSON key
Share your Sheets spreadsheet with the service account email address
This file is the "memory" of your AI — inject it into every Groq prompt:
{ "q": "Do you offer a free trial?", "a": "Yes — AcmeLite is free forever." },
{ "q": "What is your refund policy?", "a": "30-day money-back guarantee, no questions asked." },
{ "q": "Do you integrate with Slack?", "a": "Yes, native Slack integration is included in AcmePro." }
Save this as ~/email-automation/init.sql — Docker runs it automatically on first start.
-- Stores all connected email accounts (scale to multiple mailboxes)
provider VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL, -- gmail | outlook | smtp
-- NEVER store plain passwords here; use n8n credential vault
daily_send_limit INTEGER DEFAULT 450, -- 10% buffer under Gmail 500 limit
-- Central log: every email seen is recorded here for deduplication & audit
message_id VARCHAR(500) NOT NULL UNIQUE, -- RFC 2822 Message-ID header
to_addresses TEXT[], -- array for CC/BCC handling
draft_reasoning TEXT, -- CoT output from Groq
final_body TEXT, -- body after human edits
CREATE INDEX idx_email_logs_received_at ON email_logs(received_at DESC);
-- Tracks potential leads identified by the classification layer
crm_id VARCHAR(255), -- for future CRM integration
CREATE INDEX idx_leads_score ON leads(score DESC);
-- Trigger to auto-update 'updated_at' on any row change
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column();
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column();
FOR EACH ROW EXECUTE FUNCTION update_updated_at_column();
-- Seed default account (update with your real details)
There are two n8n workflows. Import via Settings → Import Workflow in the n8n UI.
Workflow A: Ingest → Classify → Draft → Google Sheets
Node 1 — IMAP Trigger (Poll for new email)
Poll Every: 60 seconds ← balance freshness vs IMAP rate limits
// Normalize the IMAP output into a clean object
(message_id, thread_id, from_address, subject, body_text, received_at, status)
Parameters: message_id, thread_id, from_address, subject, body_text, received_at
Node 6 — Function Node: Safety / Anti-Loop Check
// Load "do not reply to" list from business context (or hardcode here)
Node 7 — IF Node: Is this a loop?
TRUE → Postgres: UPDATE email_logs SET status='loop_blocked' WHERE message_id=$1 → STOP
Node 8 — HTTP Request: Groq Classification (API Call #1)
"content": "You are an email classifier. Return ONLY valid JSON with keys: category, confidence, needs_reply, reasoning. Categories: spam | inquiry | urgent | lead | newsletter | other."
// Fallback if Groq returns malformed JSON
parsed = { category: 'inquiry', confidence: 0.5, needs_reply: true, reasoning: 'parse_error' };
needs_reply: parsed.needs_reply !== false, // default to true
Node 10 — IF Node: Does it need a reply?
SELECT from_address, subject, body_text, draft_body, sent_at
Node 12 — Function Node: Build Context-Rich Prompt
// Read business context (mounted as volume in Docker)
// In n8n, use a Read Binary File node pointed to /data/business_context.json
// OR hardcode a summary here for simplicity
// Business context summary (load from file in production)
Company: Acme Solutions | Founder: Krishna | Site: acme.com
Tone: Professional, warm, concise — max 150 words
Sign-off: Best regards, Krishna | Founder, Acme Solutions
Do NOT mention competitors. Never promise features not listed.
Node 13 — HTTP Request: Groq Draft Generation (API Call #2)
{ "role": "system", "content": "{{ $json.prompt_context }}\n\nYou are a professional email assistant. Follow the FULL system prompt in Section 6 of the documentation. Return ONLY valid JSON." },
Node 14 — Postgres: Update Draft in DB
$9: sheets_row_id (row number returned by Node 15 Append Row)
NOTE: Run Node 15 first, capture returned row number, then pass into this query.
Node 15 — Google Sheets: Append Row for Approval
Sheet ID: [Your Sheet ID from URL]
G: Edited Body → (leave empty — human fills this)
Workflow B: Google Sheets Poller → SMTP Send
This second workflow polls the Sheets tab every 5 minutes and sends approved emails.
Node 2 — Google Sheets: Read Approved Rows
↑ Catches AI drafts, auto-approved, and human-edited rows
Max Rows: 10 ← process in small batches to respect rate limits
Wait: 45 seconds between batches ← Rate limiter for SMTP
Node 4 — Function Node: Determine Final Body
// If human edited the body (column G), use that; otherwise use AI draft (column F)
X-Auto-Submitted: no ← Signals this is a real human reply, not a loop
Node 7 — Google Sheets: Update Row Status
This is the master system prompt for the Groq/Llama 3 draft generation node. Copy this verbatim into your n8n Function Node or HTTP Request body.
You are an expert email assistant for Acme Solutions. Your job is to draft professional,
warm, concise email replies that sound like they were written by Krishna, the founder.
Step 1: Does this email actually need a reply?
- If it's a newsletter, promotional blast, or out-of-office: needs_reply=false
- If it contains a question, request, complaint, or interest: needs_reply=true
Step 2: What is the sender's primary goal / pain point?
Step 3: What information from our FAQs/products directly addresses their need?
Step 4: What is the ideal tone? (Urgent → faster/warmer; Lead → enticing; Inquiry → helpful)
Step 5: Draft reply in under 150 words. No fluff. No hollow phrases like "Hope this finds you well."
Input: "Hi, I saw your product. How much does it cost and is there a trial?"

"draft_body": "Hi there,

AcmeLite is completely free — no credit card, no expiry. It covers up to 3 projects and unlimited users, which is a great starting point.

If you later need advanced features, AcmePro is $99/month with a 30-day money-back guarantee.

Want me to set you up with a quick onboarding call?

Best regards,
Krishna
Founder, Acme Solutions",
"reasoning": "Sender asked about pricing and trial. Answered directly with free tier first, then paid option. Added a low-pressure CTA."
Input: "URGENT: Your app has been down for 2 hours. This is unacceptable!"

"draft_body": "Hi,

I sincerely apologize for the disruption — I understand how frustrating this must be.

I've flagged this as a Priority 1 issue and our team is investigating immediately. I'll personally update you within 30 minutes.

Thank you for alerting us.

Best regards,
Krishna
Founder, Acme Solutions",
"reasoning": "Negative sentiment, urgent flag. Apologized first, committed to timeline, added personal accountability."
Input: "You're receiving this because you subscribed to TechWeekly..."
"reasoning": "This is a mass newsletter. No action required. No reply needed."
Return ONLY a JSON object. No markdown. No preamble. No explanation outside the JSON.
Slice email body to first 2,000 chars in Node 12
Only include last 3 thread messages, not full history
One call does both classify + draft (consolidate prompts)
Cache classification for identical subjects in Redis/Postgres
Use smaller model for spam/newsletter detection; 70B only for drafts
Create a new Google Sheet named "Email Automation Dashboard". Add these sheets:
A:Timestamp, B:From, C:Subject, D:Category, E:Draft Subject, F:Draft Body, G:Edited Body (human fills), H:Status, I:Message ID
Audit trail of all sent emails
Auto-populated from Workflow B after send
Use Google Sheets formulas on EmailApprovals
Email addresses to never reply to
Single column of addresses; n8n reads this
Status Values (human enters one of these in column H):
PENDING — AI draft ready, awaiting human review (default)
APPROVED — Send the AI draft as-is (Workflow B picks this up)
EDIT — Send the content in column G instead (human edited body)
REJECTED — Do not send; mark as reviewed
ESCALATE — Flag for personal attention; n8n sends Slack/email alert
Workflow B polls column H every 5 minutes.
Only rows with status = 'APPROVED' or 'EDIT' trigger an actual email send.
For high-confidence, low-risk emails you can auto-approve without human review:
// Add this check in Node 9 (after classification) in Workflow A
// Workflow B treats AUTO_APPROVED same as APPROVED
// But all auto-approved emails appear in SentLog with flag for weekly audit
8. Operational Resilience — Rate Limiting, Tunnels & Fallback
8.1 Rate Limiting: Avoiding Gmail Spam Flags
Gmail free accounts allow 500 outbound emails per day. More critically, sending too fast triggers spam heuristics. Apply these rules:
n8n: Use "Split in Batches" with "Wait Between Batches: 45 seconds"
Effect: Max 80 emails/hour — looks human, avoids spam scoring.
RULE 2 — Daily send cap enforced in Postgres
SELECT daily_send_count, daily_send_limit FROM accounts WHERE id = $1;
After send: UPDATE accounts SET daily_send_count = daily_send_count + 1
RULE 3 — Reset daily counter at midnight
Add a second Schedule Trigger workflow:
Action: UPDATE accounts SET daily_send_count = 0, last_reset_date = CURRENT_DATE;
RULE 4 — Warm-up period for new accounts
DKIM: Enable in Gmail Admin (Google Workspace) or use free Zoho for custom domain
Access your n8n dashboard from anywhere without opening firewall ports or exposing your home IP:
# Windows (Krishna_pc — i5-1235U / 8 GB): Download the MSI installer directly:
# Run the MSI → cloudflared is added to PATH automatically.
# Then open PowerShell (or CMD) and continue from Step 2 below.
# Step 5: Add CNAME record in Cloudflare DNS
cloudflared tunnel route dns n8n-tunnel n8n.yourdomain.com
# Your n8n is now at: https://n8n.yourdomain.com (HTTPS, free SSL!)
# Basic Auth from docker-compose.yml protects it.
✅ For a no-domain quick test, run: cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:5678
Cloudflare gives you a random public URL like https://abc123.trycloudflare.com — no account needed. Perfect for demos.
8.3 Local LLM Fallback — Ollama When Internet Is Down
# Windows (Krishna_pc): Download the one-click installer from https://ollama.com/download/windows
# Run OllamaSetup.exe → Ollama starts as a background service automatically.
# No WSL or curl needed on Windows.
# Step 2: Pull Llama 3 8B (4-bit quantized — fits in 8 GB RAM)
# ⚠ On Krishna_pc (8 GB RAM / i5-1235U): use llama3:8b only — the 70B model won't fit.
ollama pull llama3:8b # Q4 quantized — needs ~5 GB RAM, ~5 sec/req on CPU
# Step 3: Ollama runs as local API on port 11434
# Step 4: In n8n, add an IF node BEFORE the Groq HTTP Request node:
# Condition: Check if Groq is reachable
# host.docker.internal works on Windows & macOS Docker Desktop automatically.
# On Linux add --add-host=host.docker.internal:host-gateway to docker run,
# Add to docker-compose.yml to auto-restart on crash:
# n8n exposes /healthz endpoint — returns 200 when healthy
# Docker will restart the container if health check fails 3 times
The most dangerous failure mode in email automation is an AI reply loop: two automated systems replying to each other indefinitely. This logic is your circuit breaker.
// SAFETY VALVE v2.0 — Multi-layer anti-loop protection
// Prevent replying to ourselves (e.g. if we CC ourselves)
// Prevent more than 5 AI replies in a single thread
// If we sent an email to this address in the last 10 minutes, block
// (requires DB query — handled in Node before this; pass flag via $json.recently_replied)
9.2 Thread Depth Check Query (add before Safety Valve node)
-- Check how many times we've replied in this thread
-- Pass reply_count as $json.thread_reply_count into Safety Valve node
10.1 Scaling on a Single Laptop (Vertical Optimization)
Exponential backoff + queue emails in Postgres with "queued" status; retry every 15 min
Rotate across 2–3 Gmail accounts; round-robin in n8n via IF node on account_id
Set N8N_EXECUTIONS_DATA_MAX_AGE=168 (prune logs >7 days) in docker-compose env
Add VACUUM schedule + partition email_logs by month using pg_partman
Enable Gmail Push Notifications via Google Cloud Pub/Sub (free tier: 10 GB/month)
10.2 Future Horizontal Scale (When You Grow Beyond Free Tier)
This architecture is designed to evolve. When you outgrow the free tier, these are clean migration paths:
n8n Cloud ($24/mo) — same workflows, no Docker management
Resend.com ($9/mo for 50k emails) — better deliverability than raw Gmail SMTP
OpenAI GPT-4o or Anthropic Claude 3 Haiku (~$0.0025/1k tokens) — still very cheap
10.3 n8n Error Handling — Retry & Dead Letter
For EVERY n8n node that makes an external call (Groq, SMTP, Sheets):
1. Click the node → Settings → "On Error" → "Continue (using error output)"
- Log error to Postgres: UPDATE email_logs SET status='failed', last_error=$1
- If HTTP 429 (rate limit): SET status='queued', retry after 15 min
- If SMTP error: SET send_attempts = send_attempts + 1
NEVER store email passwords in docker-compose.yml in plain text — use .env file with Docker secrets
n8n encrypts credentials in its internal Postgres store with AES-256
The .env file should be chmod 600 (owner read-only) and added to .gitignore
Gmail App Passwords are scoped — they can only access Gmail, not your Google account
# Create .env file instead of hardcoding passwords in docker-compose.yml
PostgreSQL data directory is local — only you have access
Add FULL-TEXT SEARCH index on body_text using pg_trgm for fast lookups without scanning all data
Implement a "right to erasure" query: DELETE FROM email_logs WHERE from_address = $1
Store unsubscribe requests in leads.unsubscribed = TRUE; check this before every outbound send
Keep email body_text only 90 days: add a daily cron to SET body_text = NULL WHERE received_at < NOW() - INTERVAL '90 days'
12.1 Key SQL Queries for Google Sheets Analytics Tab
-- Daily summary (run this as a scheduled n8n workflow → write to Sheets "Analytics" tab)
-- 2. Top senders (leads to follow up with)
SELECT from_address, COUNT(*) as email_count, AVG(sentiment_score) as avg_sentiment
-- 3. Token usage this month (track against Groq free tier)
Create a third n8n workflow (Watchdog) triggered every hour:
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM email_logs WHERE status='failed' AND updated_at > NOW()-INTERVAL '1h'
If count > 5 → send yourself an email alert via SMTP
2. PENDING approvals older than 2 hours (you forgot to check Sheets):
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM email_logs WHERE approval_status='PENDING' AND created_at < NOW()-INTERVAL '2h'
If count > 0 → send reminder email to yourself
Make a tiny test API call every 4 hours
If fails 2x → switch n8n workflow to use Ollama fallback
Add rows to accounts table; one n8n workflow per account using account_id routing
n8n workflow: +10 points on inquiry, +25 on reply, -5 on no-open; store in leads.score
n8n workflow reads CSV from Google Sheets; sends with 45s delay between each
n8n Read Binary File node; store file path in email_logs; never pass attachments to Groq
Add "language": "Spanish" to classification JSON; inject language instruction into draft prompt
Google Cloud Pub/Sub free tier + Gmail watch API → n8n webhook → no more polling lag
n8n HubSpot free tier node or direct API call to Notion/Airtable free tier
Random IF node splits drafts 50/50; track open rates via URL tracking pixel in Google Sheets
Weekly Postgres query; email yourself if avg_sentiment drops below -0.3
Detect "unsubscribe" keyword; auto-set leads.unsubscribed=TRUE; send confirmation
✅ The only non-zero cost would be electricity (~$2-5/month to keep the laptop powered 24/7) and your existing internet bill. OpEx from software and APIs: $0.00.
Install Docker Desktop → docker compose up -d
Create Groq account → copy API key → add to n8n credentials
Create Google Cloud project → enable Sheets API → download service account key
Copy business_context.json template → fill in your company details
Create the email_automation Postgres DB (auto-done via init.sql)
Import Workflow A and Workflow B JSON into n8n
Install Cloudflare tunnel (optional) → access dashboard remotely
Install Ollama + llama3:8b (optional) → offline fallback ready
Send yourself a test email → watch it flow through the system in n8n executions log
Designed for Krishna · Zero-Cost Email Automation System · Built with n8n + Groq + PostgreSQL
